bridge_status_interpretation: After graph-aware bridge hardening and no-launch reparse, the bridge observed completion, final marker, runSubagent, runtime outputs, required commands, one repeated validation command, and one benign runtime Test-Path check with no deviations.
learned_changes: Graph-derived tickets should separate full graph context from executable supervisor and worker node IDs.
Audit tickets should include deterministically pre-extracted source facts instead of asking the free supervisor to copy small JSON fields manually.
The auditor role must explicitly map parse-failed, blocked, unreadable, and missing-evidence gates to needs_coordinator_review.
Graph tickets should use a self-contained PYTHONPATH plus python -m agent_workbench.cli validation command instead of relying on agent-workbench being on PATH.

The bridge command-diff logic needs graph-aware tolerance for repair-loop validation reruns and benign file-existence checks; this was implemented and verified by reparsing the v4 session without relaunching Copilot.
interpretation: This is positive evidence that a local Copilot supervisor can execute a small FreshForge-compatible graph-shaped workflow with a read-only subagent, deterministic pre-extracted facts, bounded repair, and authority validation. The next gap is bridge acceptance logic and larger workflow scale, not basic feasibility.
